section: Front matter: title, abstract, keywords, PDF metadata, and final organization paragraph
intent: Finalize the report front matter after the main chapters, figures, tables, appendices, and conclusions have been stabilized. The proposal retitles the report, replaces the abstract with a 232-word bounded summary of the implemented-model audit and diagnostic 1D-3D velocity comparison, updates printed and PDF keywords, aligns PDF title/subject metadata with the completed study, and aligns the final organization paragraph with the completed chapter structure.

replacement_tex.frontmatter/abstract.tex:
\medskip
\noindent\textbf{Abstract.}\par\smallskip
This report audits a reduced-order stenosis solver and a diagnostic comparison
with available resolved 3D velocity data. The implemented solver is the Julia
\path{StenosisHemodynamics} realization of an $R_{\max}$-normalized
Canic-derived one-dimensional area--flow model. Physical circular area and flow
are represented in solver coordinates by $a=R^2$ and
$q=Q_{\mathrm{phys}}/\pi$, so $A_{\mathrm{phys}}=\pi a$,
$Q_{\mathrm{phys}}=\pi q$, and the mean velocity is $q/a$. The principal
comparison realization uses the declared parabolic-profile baseline, Newtonian
rheology, a fixed-area characteristic boundary approximation, MUSCL finite
volume, minmod-limited states, Rusanov flux, source splitting, and native
SSPRK3.

The verification record gives positive but bounded manufactured-solution
evidence for the declared forced operator. It also exposes the central numerical
limitation: the current MUSCL/Rusanov realization does not preserve the
geometry-rest equilibrium. In zero-forcing C23/C40 rest runs, the artificial
flow retained at $t=1$ s is the same order as the production comparison-flow
scale. The comparison with resolved data is therefore interpreted only through a
declared plane--tetrahedron quadrature operator that computes section area,
physical flow, and area-mean axial velocity. Under that operator, the reported
C23/C40 section and radial-profile results are descriptive velocity
discrepancies for the available cases. Their interpretation is limited by
unmatched or unpersisted 3D wall, boundary, material, history, and
current/deformed geometry information, by unresolved axial variation in
extracted 3D flow, and by the $1.0$ s versus $0.9995$ s sample-time offset.
replacement_tex.sections/01-intro/index.tex report organ:
\begin{itemize}
    \item Section~\ref{sec:background-literature-review} reviews only the
    background needed for stenosis-aware 1D closures, well-balanced behavior,
    implementation verification, and cross-dimensional velocity observables.
    \item Section~\ref{sec:methodology} specifies the model variables,
    solver-coordinate convention, closures, boundary approximation,
    finite-volume/time-integration realization, comparison operator, metrics,
    and provenance record.
    \item Section~\ref{sec:numerical-verification} reports bounded MMS evidence
    and the non-well-balanced geometry-rest drift that constrains the later
    comparison.
    \item Section~\ref{sec:resolved-3d-comparison} reports the C23/C40 velocity
    and physical-flow discrepancies under the declared observation operator and
    states the unmatched 3D conditions that limit interpretation.
    \item Sections~\ref{sec:discussion} and
    \ref{sec:conclusions-limitations} answer the three research questions and
    identify the next required step: an equilibrium-preserving discretization
    followed by matched 3D metadata and production-sensitivity records.
\end{itemize}

risk: Front-matter summaries are necessarily compact; the patch avoids adding new numerical values and anchors the abstract in the approved claim ledger.
The title, keywords, and PDF metadata change report framing but do not alter labels, citations, equations, generated data, or manuscript structure.
PDF metadata uses PDF-safe plain hyphenated 1D-3D text while the printed TeX title and keyword list retain their D18 typesetting.
The organization paragraph changes only cross-reference prose; all referenced section labels already exist in the current source.
The abstract states descriptive C23/C40 discrepancy status without restating conflicted or quarantined radial numeric table values.
